title: M-O-R-S_REFERENCE-SYSTEM
content:
M-O-R-S_REFERENCE-SYSTEM


Creating a universal reference system that can unify software, hardware, text, video, music, photos, and any digital media requires a structured, hierarchical, and flexible approach. Below is a proposed system based on a hybrid of Dewey Decimal Classification (DDC), UUIDs, and metadata tagging:

1. Core Structure: Media Object Reference System (MORS)

Each media object is assigned a MORS identifier, formatted as:

{Category}-{Subcategory}-{Format}-{Unique ID}-{Version}-{Metadata Hash}

Example:

TXT-BOOK-PDF-000001-01-AE3F2D
VID-MOVIE-MP4-003476-02-B12C7A
IMG-PHOTO-JPG-100984-01-8F4D9C

2. Categories (Primary Classification)

Each object belongs to a primary category:

Code Category Description
TXT Text Books, articles, scripts, manuscripts
VID Video Movies, TV shows, home videos, VR content
IMG Image Photos, illustrations, design files
MUS Music Songs, instrumentals, scores
